resources.string: Could not open the privacy policy. Check that a browser is available.
Preparing trainer for telemetry-only free ride
First answer captured. Hide or re-show the probe to ask again.
Connection is taking longer than expected
Could not connect to the trainer yet. Check that the trainer is powered on and nearby.
Could not open the link. Check that a browser is available.
The built-in guided workout included in Free. Load it as your workout for this session.
Set FTP and baseline HR profile before connecting devices.
Select trainer and optional HR source, then confirm connection quality.
Choose how you want to prepare the workout for this session.
Load a workout or use the editor to create or edit one.
AI-driven workout modes are listed for upcoming phases.
Manual ride without a structured workout. The app still records telemetry and shows session summary.
Review setup details and start only when trainer + workout are ready.
Fix workout import issues before continuing.
Resolve workout execution issues before continuing.
Link a workout folder before folder-based import or export.
No supported workout files (.zwo, .xml, or .ewo) found in selected folder.
Could not persist folder permission. Select the folder again.
Folder write failed. Falling back to system file picker.
Included workouts stay read-only. Open one in the editor to save your own copy.
No built-in workouts found in packaged assets.
FTP is the highest power you can sustain for 60 minutes.
You can update this later if you get a better estimate.
Age %1$d • Sex not set, HR zone uses estimate
Set age to enable HR zone. Sex improves the estimate.
Age and sex improve HR zone estimates but do not block trainer setup.
Use the ramp test if you do not know your FTP yet.
Debug-only mode. Start without trainer MAC and stream synthetic trainer telemetry.
Wake the trainer before scanning and keep it nearby during setup.
Scanning for nearby devices. Stay on this screen until the list updates.
Run this if you want to confirm FTMS control before your first ride.
Select a trainer first, then return here to continue.
Select a trainer before running compatibility check.
Bluetooth permission is required to run compatibility check.
Compatibility check is running. Keep trainer awake and nearby.
%1$s Artifacts could not be persisted locally.
trainer did not grant control in time
could not start control request to trainer
could not take control of trainer
trainer did not respond to target power command in time
could not send target power command to trainer
trainer did not respond to stop command in time
could not start fallback cleanup command
Controls whether summary FIT files are auto-saved, prompted, or skipped.
Scan permission is required to search devices.
Bluetooth permission is required to connect the heart rate monitor.
No matching devices found. Keep trainer/strap powered on and try again.
Device scan could not be started.
Could not connect to the saved trainer. Confirm the trainer is powered on. Do you want to search for devices again?
Trainer rejected control request (code %1$d). Confirm trainer is powered on and not controlled by another app. Search for devices again?
Trainer did not confirm control request in time. Confirm trainer is powered on and try searching devices again.
Bluetooth permission is required to start a session. Open app settings and allow Nearby devices permission.
Fix workout import issues before starting.
Resolve workout execution issues before starting.
Recovery looks limited today. Consider reducing intensity.
Run a ramp test to measure your FTP from live power output.
This guided FTP ramp test estimates your current FTP from live trainer power data. It is designed for riders who have not done a ramp test before as well as repeat testers.

The protocol starts with a 5-minute warm-up, then moves into 1-minute steps that get harder every minute. The test ends when you stop the ramp with the button or your cadence collapses below 30 rpm for about 10 seconds.
Ride the early minutes smoothly, then keep pedaling as the load rises. For the most useful result, continue until the final steps feel genuinely unsustainable, not just uncomfortable.
After you press Start Test, the trainer prepares first. The warm-up timer does not start until you begin pedaling.
Start pedaling to begin the warm-up timer
The test is ready, but the countdown stays paused until live cadence or power is detected.
Less than 6 ramp steps were completed. Ride harder and longer next time.
ERG control was lost mid-test. Attempt cancelled.
No live power signal. Connect your trainer first.
Trainer control could not be granted. You can continue in advisory mode where the target is shown as guidance, but pacing is manual.
Not saved to .ewo — used for preview and IF/TSS
These values improve preview accuracy and zone-based metrics, but they are not written into the workout file.
Fix the listed validation issues first. Save stays disabled until blocking errors are cleared.
Keep rider-facing labels short and readable at a glance.
Use HR for an absolute working band when you already know the target in bpm.
Use the HR-relative target when the band should follow the selected reference point instead of fixed bpm.
Built-in workout opened. Saving from the editor creates a copy in your folder.
Cannot export: document has validation errors
Workout has HR targets but no control block is set
Workout-level HR control and safety limits
Chart, compile notes, and compiled steps
Use this panel to spot unrealistic targets, warnings, or compile issues before export.
Create, preview and export a workout without leaving the app.
Edit ZWO XML directly. Save/apply is disabled until XML is valid.
Power values are fractions (for example 0.75 = 75%%). Structured mapping currently accepts 30%%-200%%.
Save/apply requires valid XML and only supported structured steps.
Add at least one step to start editing.
Fix source XML issues before save/apply:
Loaded selected workout. Unsupported steps skipped: %1$d.
Fix validation errors before applying to menu.
Fix source XML errors before switching to Structured mode.
Unsupported source steps detected: %1$d. Remove or replace them in source mode before save/apply.
Saving to the configured Documents folder overwrites the same filename. Picker fallback may create a new copy.
This draft has unsaved changes. Do you want to save it before applying to menu?
You have unsaved changes. Apply this draft to menu, discard changes, or keep editing.
Mock mode active. Telemetry is synthetic.
HR control active. Current heart rate %1$s. Target band %2$d-%3$d bpm. Adjustment range %4$d-%5$d watts.
Trainer command is secondary during HR control. Instant power %1$s. Current trainer command %2$s.
Connection is unstable. Keep effort steady while the link recovers.
Power drift is %1$d%% from target. Smooth effort toward target.
Cadence is %1$d rpm. Increase cadence now toward target (%2$d).
Cadence is %1$d rpm. Reduce cadence slightly toward target (%2$d).
Heart rate is %1$d bpm. Reduce effort now.
Great work. You completed the planned workout.
Session timestamps are invalid for FIT export.
FIT share failed while preparing the file URI.
No app available to share the FIT file.
Automatic FIT save enabled for future sessions.
FIT export confirmation will be shown for each session.
FIT export disabled for future sessions.
Automatic FIT save failed. Check Documents folder access.
Choose how FIT files should be handled after sessions.
FIT file is saved automatically after each session.
FIT export is disabled by your preference.
Recovery guidance suggests a lighter next session.
Use import for an existing workout file. Use the editor when you want to build or repair one.
A default folder is optional, but it makes repeated import and export faster.
Open it in the editor and replace unsupported parts with structured steps.
Review the preview before starting, especially if the file came from another app.
Workout starts now. Settle in and let the opening minutes come to you.
Main interval block starts now. Keep the hard reps controlled and use the easier steps to reset.
Next block starts now. Settle into the target and keep the effort even.
Build block starts now. Let the effort rise smoothly without forcing it.
Recovery block starts now. Bring the effort down and prepare for the next change.
Cooldown starts now. Let the effort come down gradually to the finish.
